questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50° and angle B measures 60°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in any triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 50° − 60° = 70°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle measuring 35°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: One angle is 90° (right angle), another is 35°. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the two equal sides meet at a vertex angle of 40°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 40°, then 2b + 40° = 180°, so each base angle b = 70°.

questions.question: Two angles in a triangle measure 45° and 45°. Is this triangle a right triangle? Explain.
questions.explanation: The third angle = 180° − 45° − 45° = 90°. Since one angle is 90°, this is a right triangle.

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal. What is the measure of each angle?
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are equal. Since 3a = 180°, each angle a = 60°.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = 62° and angle Q = 58°. Which angle is the largest in the triangle?
questions.explanation: Angle R = 180° − 62° − 58° = 60°. Since 62° > 60° > 58°, angle P is the largest, but the question asks which angle is largest: the largest angle measure is at vertex P. Correction: R = 60°, so P = 62° is largest. Answer should be P, but re-reading: R = 60° is smallest. The largest is P at 62°.
